text:
Centre for Epilepsy

Neurosciences Division 

Clinic Date: 12 September 2019 

Dr Ahmed Khan  
Centre for Epilepsy  
Queen Square, London WC1N 3BG  

Dear Dr Ahmed Khan,                                                                                                         

Daniel Hart, DOB: 22-03-1989, Hospital No: H382019 NHS No. 9076543210  
Flat 3, 27 Marlborough Road, London, N19 4JP  

Epilepsy 

Medication: Lamotrigine maintained since February 2019; Clobazam introduced as rescue in April 2019.

Lamotrigine 150mg twice daily  
Clobazam 10mg at night as needed around periods of increased seizure activity (patient-reported usage approximately once weekly in June, otherwise intermittent)

Vagus Nerve Stimulator (VNS): Implanted 2017. No parameter changes at this review. Patient uses magnet abortively; reports transient throat paresthesia with stimulation which is tolerable.

Lamotrigine Blood Level: 

28th August 2019: 4.8 mg/L (laboratory reference 1.0–15.0 mg/L). Dose 150mg twice daily.

Seizures: He had a cluster of three seizures in June (short, not full convulsions, fluctuating awareness, self-terminating). In July he had a nocturnal seizure, and in August a single tonic seizure was recorded during respite care. He reports reliable post-event fatigue without injury, no tongue biting in June events, and a brief right shoulder bruise following the August event. Family confirms magnet swipe used during the June cluster with perceived shortening of episodes.

I reviewed Daniel Hart today in person with his support worker present. He feels overall control is similar to earlier in the year. Sleep has been irregular during a house move in late May, which may have contributed to the June activity. He denies new focal neurological symptoms, mood change, or medication side effects beyond occasional dizziness on rapid standing. Adherence was discussed; he uses a dosette box and phone reminders. No intercurrent illness reported. We checked the VNS interrogation log; battery status is adequate and lead impedance is stable. No adjustments were made. Equipment care and magnet use were reinforced. A medication blister-pack refill plan was agreed with his community pharmacy. 

Plan: 
- Continue Lamotrigine 150mg twice daily.  
- Continue Clobazam 10mg nocte as needed during anticipated high-risk periods (e.g., sleep disruption), not more than 2–3 consecutive nights without review.  
- Maintain current VNS parameters; magnet swipe at onset as previously instructed.  
- Safety: nocturnal supervision maintained during respite; continue use of soft headboard and showering rather than bathing.  
- Bloods: repeat Lamotrigine level and U&E/LFTs in 3 months or sooner if clinical change.  
- Triggers: prioritise regular sleep and hydration; support worker to keep seizure diary.  
- Follow-up: nurse-led telephone check in 8 weeks; face-to-face review in 4–6 months or earlier if increase in frequency or severity.

Yours sincerely

entities.text: He had a cluster of three seizures in June... In July he had a nocturnal seizure, and in August a single tonic seizure was recorded

He had a cluster of three seizures in June (short, not full convulsions, fluctuating awareness, self-terminating). In July he had a nocturnal seizure, and in August a single tonic seizure was recorded during respite care

check__Seizure Frequency Number.analysis: The letter provides specific seizure events over recent months: a cluster of three seizures in June, one nocturnal seizure in July, and one tonic seizure in August. That totals 5 seizures across 3 months (June, July, August). There is no indication of complete seizure freedom for 6 months. Applying the rule to compute frequency from explicit counts over a stated period: 5 seizures per 3 months.
